_meta.note: All bands stored as ANNUAL taxable-income thresholds with marginal rates (%). Each country's figures are verified against the cited authority on the 'verified' date. This file is the single source of truth — the generator renders pages from it, so accuracy is audited here, not in HTML.
_meta.disclaimer: General information only, not tax advice. Verify with the named authority or a licensed adviser.

kenya.name: Kenya
notes: Personal relief of KES 28,800/year is a credit against tax, so PAYE is nil for the lowest earners.
Insurance relief (15% of qualifying premiums, up to KES 60,000/year) may also apply on top of personal relief.
This is PAYE income tax only — NSSF, SHIF (2.75% of gross) and the Affordable Housing Levy (1.5%) further reduce take-home pay.

nigeria.name: Nigeria
notes: The first ₦800,000 of annual income is tax-free under the 2026 reform (up from ₦300,000).
Rent relief = 20% of annual rent, capped at ₦500,000/year, deducted from income before tax.
New progressive bands took effect 1 January 2026, replacing the old Consolidated Relief Allowance system — many older calculators still show the outdated bands.

south_africa.name: South Africa
notes: The primary rebate of R17,820 is a credit against tax, so you pay no income tax below roughly R99,000/year.
Rates shown are for taxpayers under 65; those aged 65+ and 75+ receive larger rebates.
Excludes UIF and medical scheme tax credits, which further adjust the final figure.

ghana.name: Ghana
notes: The first GHS 5,880/year (about GHS 490/month) is tax-free.
Bands apply to chargeable income after reliefs — SSNIT (5.5% employee) is deducted before PAYE, so enter it above.
PAYE income tax only; other statutory contributions are separate.

australia.name: Australia
notes: The tax-free threshold is A$18,200; these are the resident Stage-3 rates, unchanged from 2024–25.
Excludes the 2% Medicare levy, which is calculated separately on taxable income.
Excludes offsets such as the low-income tax offset (LITO).

united_kingdom.name: United Kingdom
notes: The £12,570 personal allowance is shown as the 0% band. It tapers above £100,000 (reduced £1 for every £2 over, gone at £125,140), so effective tax is higher between £100k and £125k — verify that range with HMRC.
Rates are for England, Wales and Northern Ireland; Scotland has its own bands.
Excludes National Insurance contributions, which are separate.
